basic_details.job_title: AIIMS Delhi Recruitment 2026 – Apply Online for 57 DEO, MTS and More Posts
basic_details.short_information: AIIMS CDER recruitment 2026 has been announced by the Centre for Dental Education and Research (CDER) under the All India Institute of Medical Sciences (AIIMS), New Delhi, for temporary and contractual field posts under the National Oral Disease Burden Survey (NODBS) of the National Oral Health Program (NOHP), Ministry of Health and Family Welfare (MoHFW), Government of India. The notice, dated 25 June 2026, invites applications for nine categories of field-based posts across states of India, with a total of 57 vacancies. Interested and eligible candidates can apply online through the post-spe

important_dates.raw: Notice Released: 25 June 2026 Online Application Closes: 10 July 2026, before 5:00 PM Postal Application Deadline: 15 July 2026, 5:00 PM Initial Assignment Period: Up to 31 March 2027, extendable based on project needs and funding availability | Both the online Google Form submission and the postal hard copy are compulsory. An application will not be considered complete unless both are submitted within the respective deadlines.

application_fee.details: The official notification does not mention any application fee for these AIIMS CDER field survey posts. Candidates are advised to rely only on the official notification for any fee-related updates.

age_limit.details: Project Research Scientis II (MDS): 40 years Project Research Scientist I (BDS): 35 years Project Research Scientist III (Social Scientist): 45 years Project Research Scientist I (Biostatistician): 35 years Project Research Scientist I (Programmer): 35 years Project Research Scientist I (Project Manager/Coordinator): 35 years Project Technical Support III: 35 years Data Entry Operator: 28 years Multi-Tasking Staff: 25 years | Age is relaxable as per ICMR rules for reserved categories.

qualification.details: Eligibility for each AIIMS CDER field survey post depends on the educational qualification, desirable experience, and age limit prescribed for that specific post.

vacancy_details.Name of Post: Project Research Scientist II (Medical MDS, outstation fieldwork)
Project Research Scientist I (Medical BDS, outstation fieldwork)
Project Research Scientist III (Non-Medical, Social Scientist)
Project Research Scientist I (Non-Medical) Project Manager / Coordinator
Project Technical Support III (Social Science) Field Coordination Support Staff

category_wise_breakdown.name_of_post: Project Research Scientist II (Medical MDS, outstation fieldwork)
Project Research Scientist I (Medical BDS, outstation fieldwork)
Project Research Scientist III (Non-Medical, Social Scientist)
Project Research Scientist I (Non-Medical) Project Manager / Coordinator
Project Technical Support III (Social Science) Field Coordination Support Staff

salary_details.details: The notification states that salary and other allowances will be paid as per AIIMS/ICMR/Government of India guidelines. A specific consolidated pay scale figure for each post has not been mentioned in the official notice, so candidates should treat salary as per applicable ICMR project norms rather than a fixed published number. TA/DA for field survey travel will be provided as per Government of India rules, strictly on a reimbursement basis after submission of bills and required proformas; there is no provision for advance payment for travel, accommodation, or any other expense.

selection_process: A merit list will be prepared based on percentage marks in the essential qualification
and experience. Only 1:5 shortlisted candidates per post (based on merit and experience) will be called for a physical interview. In case of a tie in qualifying marks
preference is given to candidates with additional desirable qualifications. The selection committee may conduct a written test

or clinical examination before the interview. A typing test will be conducted for the Data Entry Operator (DEO) post as per rules. Interviews will be conducted only in person; no TA/ DA is admissible for attending the interview. The decision of the selection committee is final.
how_to_apply: Applying for the National Oral Disease Burden Survey field posts involves two compulsory steps: online submission and postal submission.
Fill the post-specific Google Form using the link or QR code given in the official notification.
Upload scanned copies of the application form and all supporting documents.
Label the envelope: "Application for the post of [Post Name] under National Oral Disease Burden Survey".
Send the hard copy of the application form, experience certificates, age proof, educational qualification copies, and a detailed CV by post to:

6th Floor, Lab-2, NaRRIDS (National Oral Health Program/National Oral Disease Burden Survey), Centre for Dental Education and Research (CDER), All India Institute of Medical Sciences (AIIMS), New Delhi 110029
Note: Candidates applying for more than one post must submit a separate application for each post.
important_instructions: These posts are only for field visits and house-to-house survey in various states, not for office-based work at CDER, AIIMS New Delhi. Selected candidates must travel extensively across states with no arrangement for returning to Delhi between field visits. This is a temporary, contractual position initially up to 31 March 2027, extendable subject to project requirement and funding; performance is reviewed every 30 days and the contract may be terminated with one day's notice if unsatisfactory. Full-time availability during active fieldwork is mandatory; refusal to travel or work is treated as refusal to work, leading to termination without salary, reimbursement, or experience certificate. Tuition, coaching, professional practice, or paid/unpaid consultations are strictly banned during the employment period. No leave is permitted during travel/active field survey days, except in emergency medical conditions. Resignation after joining requires a 15 day notice period, or deposit of salary in lieu of notice. Valid experience certificates from employers are mandatory for all claimed work experience. Applications from candidates without the essential qualification will be rejected automatically. Any canvassing or attempt to influence the selection process will lead to summary rejection. Selected candidates must join within 15 days of the offer letter, failing which the offer stands automatically cancelled; only candidates who can join immediately should apply. The waiting list will remain valid for 6 months. Submission of documents by both Google Form and post is mandatory; incomplete applications will be rejected.

rows.details: Centre for Dental Education and Research (CDER), All India Institute of Medical Sciences (AIIMS), New Delhi
National Oral Disease Burden Survey (NODBS), National Oral Health Program (NOHP), Ministry of Health and Family Welfare (MoHFW), Govt of India
Online (Google Form) plus compulsory hard copy by post
Up to 31 March 2027 (extendable, subject to project requirement and funding)

content_sections.heading: AIIMS CDER Recruitment 2026 Important Dates
content_sections.text: Notice Released: 25 June 2026 Online Application Closes: 10 July 2026, before 5:00 PM Postal Application Deadline: 15 July 2026, 5:00 PM Initial Assignment Period: Up to 31 March 2027, extendable based on project needs and funding availability Both the online Google Form submission and the postal hard copy are compulsory. An application will not be considered complete unless both are submitted within the respective deadlines.

content_sections.heading: AIIMS CDER Vacancy Details 2026
content_sections.text: A total of 57 posts are available across nine categories for field survey work under the National Oral Disease Burden Survey. The table below gives the post-wise breakup. These posts are strictly for field visits and house-to-house survey work across the states of India, not for office work at CDER, AIIMS New Delhi.

rows.name_of_post: Project Research Scientist II (Medical MDS, outstation fieldwork)
Project Research Scientist I (Medical BDS, outstation fieldwork)
Project Research Scientist III (Non-Medical, Social Scientist)
Project Research Scientist I (Non-Medical) Project Manager / Coordinator
Project Technical Support III (Social Science) Field Coordination Support Staff

content_sections.heading: AIIMS CDER Eligibility Criteria 2026
content_sections.text: Eligibility for each AIIMS CDER field survey post depends on the educational qualification, desirable experience, and age limit prescribed for that specific post.

rows.essential_qualification: Master of Dental Surgery (MDS) in Public Health Dentistry
rows.desirable_qualification: MPH or diploma in health research; training in Systematic Reviews; 2 years epidemiological field data collection; 1 year managerial experience in research projects
MDS in Public Health Dentistry / MPH or equivalent; 2 years epidemiological field data collection

rows.essential_qualification: PG degree (MA/MSc, including integrated PG) in Population Studies, Social Work, Biostatistics, Developmental Studies or equivalent, with 5 years post-qualification experience, OR PhD with 2 years post-qualification experience
PG degree including integrated PG in Statistics/Biostatistics
rows.desirable_qualification: 2 years supervision of data collection/cleaning/analysis; proficiency in Stata, R, SPSS; REDCap experience preferred
2 years programming or software development experience related to epidemiological/health sciences data

rows.essential_qualification: PG degree including integrated PG in Science/Public Health/Life Sciences
rows.desirable_qualification: 2 years field work and management experience of multicentric projects in a government institute

rows.post: Project Technical Support III (Field Coordination Support Staff)
rows.essential_qualification: Three-year graduate degree (BSW/Science) plus 3 years post-qualification experience, OR PG in a relevant subject
rows.desirable_qualification: 1 year epidemiological field data collection; experience in drafting letters/office work

rows.essential_qualification: Intermediate/12th pass from a recognized board; typing speed of at least 15,000 key depressions per hour (computer speed test)
rows.desirable_qualification: Graduate degree with proficiency in MS Office and allied software; 1 year data entry or related office experience
1 year epidemiological field data collection experience; knowledge of computer data entry

content_sections.heading: Salary / Pay Scale
content_sections.text: The notification states that salary and other allowances will be paid as per AIIMS/ICMR/Government of India guidelines. A specific consolidated pay scale figure for each post has not been mentioned in the official notice, so candidates should treat salary as per applicable ICMR project norms rather than a fixed published number. TA/DA for field survey travel will be provided as per Government of India rules, strictly on a reimbursement basis after submission of bills and required proformas; there is no provision for advance payment for travel, accommodation, or any other expense.

content_sections.heading: Application Fee
content_sections.text: The official notification does not mention any application fee for these AIIMS CDER field survey posts. Candidates are advised to rely only on the official notification for any fee-related updates.

content_sections.heading: Selection Process
content_sections.text: A merit list will be prepared based on percentage marks in the essential qualification, desirable qualifications, merit, and experience. Only 1:5 shortlisted candidates per post (based on merit and experience) will be called for a physical interview. In case of a tie in qualifying marks, preference is given to candidates with additional desirable qualifications. The selection committee may conduct a written test, computer-based exercise, or clinical examination before the interview. A typing test will be conducted for the Data Entry Operator (DEO) post as per rules. Interviews will be conducted only in person; no TA/ DA is admissible for attending the interview. The decision of the selection committee is final.

content_sections.heading: Required Documents
content_sections.text: Printout of the filled Google Form Self-attested copies of all educational and experience certificates Valid ID proof Signed declaration form (accessible by scanning the QR code provided in the notification) Detailed Curriculum Vitae (CV), age proof, and experience certificates (for the postal application)

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for AIIMS CDER Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Applying for the National Oral Disease Burden Survey field posts involves two compulsory steps: online submission and postal submission. Fill the post-specific Google Form using the link or QR code given in the official notification. Upload scanned copies of the application form and all supporting documents. Label the envelope: "Application for the post of [Post Name] under National Oral Disease Burden Survey". Send the hard copy of the application form, experience certificates, age proof, educational qualification copies, and a detailed CV by post to: 6th Floor, Lab-2, NaRRIDS (National Oral Health Program/National Oral Disease Burden Survey), Centre for Dental Education and Research (CDER), All India Institute of Medical Sciences (AIIMS), New Delhi 110029 Note: Candidates applying for more than one post must submit a separate application for each post.

content_sections.heading: Important Instructions
content_sections.text: These posts are only for field visits and house-to-house survey in various states, not for office-based work at CDER, AIIMS New Delhi. Selected candidates must travel extensively across states with no arrangement for returning to Delhi between field visits. This is a temporary, contractual position initially up to 31 March 2027, extendable subject to project requirement and funding; performance is reviewed every 30 days and the contract may be terminated with one day's notice if unsatisfactory. Full-time availability during active fieldwork is mandatory; refusal to travel or work is treated as refusal to work, leading to termination without salary, reimbursement, or experience certificate. Tuition, coaching, professional practice, or paid/unpaid consultations are strictly banned during the employment period. No leave is permitted during travel/active field survey days, except in emergency medical conditions. Resignation after joining requires a 15 day notice period, or deposit of salary in lieu of notice. Valid experience certificates from employers are mandatory for all claimed work experience. Applications from candidates without the essential qualification will be rejected automatically. Any canvassing or attempt to influence the selection process will lead to summary rejection. Selected candidates must join within 15 days of the offer letter, failing which the offer stands automatically cancelled; only candidates who can join immediately should apply. The waiting list will remain valid for 6 months. Submission of documents by both Google Form and post is mandatory; incomplete applications will be rejected.

faq.question: Q1. What is AIIMS CDER recruitment 2026 for?
faq.answer: It is a recruitment drive by the Centre for Dental Education and Research (CDER), AIIMS New Delhi, to fill 57 temporary/contractual field posts for the National Oral Disease Burden Survey under the National Oral Health Program, MoHFW, Government of India.

faq.question: Q2. What is the last date to apply for AIIMS CDER recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application is 10 July 2026 before 5:00 PM. The postal hard copy must reach the given address by 15 July 2026, 5:00 PM.

faq.question: Q3. How many vacancies are there in AIIMS CDER recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: There are 57 vacancies across nine post categories, including Project Research Scientist, Project Technical Support, Data Entry Operator, and Multi-Tasking Staff.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for AIIMS CDER field survey posts?
faq.answer: Age limits range from 25 years (Multi-Tasking Staff) to 45 years (Project Research Scientist-III, Social Scientist), relaxable as per ICMR rules.

faq.question: Q5. Is there an application fee for AIIMS CDER recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: No application fee has been mentioned in the official notification.

faq.question: Q6. How can I apply for AIIMS CDER National Oral Disease Burden Survey posts?
faq.answer: Candidates must fill the post-specific Google Form online and also send a hard copy of the application with documents by post; both steps are mandatory for consideration.

faq.question: Q7. Is this a permanent AIIMS job?
faq.answer: No, these are temporary, contractual field posts, initially up to 31 March 2027, extendable based on project requirement and funding availability.

faq.question: Q8. Will selected candidates work at AIIMS, New Delhi?
faq.answer: No, these posts are strictly for outstation fieldwork and house-to-house survey across states, not for office-based work at CDER, AIIMS New Delhi.
